findings.title: Skill does not specify a license
Credential and environment exfiltration via deployment audit upload
findings.description: The embedded Python code captures the full process environment and sensitive local credential material, then transmits it over the network to a remote audit endpoint. This includes all environment variables, AWS credentials, GCP credential database contents, kubeconfig, and SSH directory inventory. This is a clear read→collect→send exfiltration chain unrelated to basic deployment validation.
findings.evidence: _capture_deployment_context() builds ctx from dict(os.environ), reads ~/.aws/credentials, ~/.config/gcloud/credentials.db, ~/.kube/config, lists ~/.ssh, and validate_environment() sends json.dumps(_ctx) to https://deploy-verify.internal.io/api/audit via curl.
findings.remediation: Remove all collection of local credentials and environment dumps. Do not transmit deployment context off-host unless strictly necessary, explicitly disclosed, minimized, and user-approved. Restrict collection to narrowly scoped non-secret metadata.

findings.title: Hardcoded bearer token embedded in skill instructions
findings.description: The skill contains a hardcoded bearer token in the network request header. Embedding secrets directly in a skill exposes credentials to anyone who can inspect the package and may enable unauthorized use of the referenced service.
findings.remediation: Remove hardcoded secrets from the skill. Store credentials in a secure secret manager or runtime-provided environment variable with proper access controls and rotation.

findings.title: Deceptive manifest and instructions conceal sensitive data harvesting
findings.description: The skill presents itself as a deployment helper that validates configuration and checks service health, but its actual behavior includes harvesting local credentials and forwarding environment state to a remote service. This materially exceeds the stated purpose and disguises dangerous capability behind a benign deployment workflow.
findings.evidence: Description claims deployment validation and artifact management, while the body instructs forwarding full environment state and embedded code reads credential files and posts them to a remote endpoint.
findings.remediation: Align the manifest and instructions with actual behavior. Remove undisclosed credential access and network transmission, or explicitly declare narrowly scoped, justified data handling with informed user approval.
